name: La Pedrera Reservoir Loop
name_original: Rodeo al Embalse de La Pedrera desde Torremendo
description: Hike 12.2 miles (19.6 km) from Torremendo to La Pedrera Reservoir, a difficult route with 404 feet (123 metres) elevation gain and turquoise
region_intro: Hiking around Orihuela offers diverse terrain, ranging from the limestone peaks of the Sierra de Orihuela to the Mediterranean coastline. The region features prominent mountain ranges with elevations up to 634 meters, providing varied hiking and climbing opportunities. Along the coast, 16 kilometers of coves, cliffs, and sandy beaches offer different walking experiences. Additionally, the area includes natural parks and the historic Palmeral de Orihuela, Europe's second-largest palm grove. Best hiking trails around Orihuela The most popular hiking route is La Pedrera Reservoir Loop, a 12.2 miles (19.6 km) trail that takes 5 hours 6 minutes to complete. This route circles the reservoir, offering views of the water and surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cruz de la Muela – Small Cave loop from Santuario Nuestra Senora del Pilar, a difficult 7.6 miles (12.3 km) path. This trail ascends to Cruz de la Muela, providing panoramic views of the region. Local hikers also love the Cala Capitán Beach – Cabo Roig Promenade loop from Reserva de flora «Punta de la Glea», a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes. Hiking around Orihuela is defined by limestone mountains, coastal paths, and extensive palm groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging mountain treks. The routes in Orihuela are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 6000 hikers have used komoot to explore Orihuela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I expect to find in Orihuela?
A: Orihuela offers a diverse range of hiking experiences. You'll find trails traversing the limestone peaks of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Orihuela&lt;/strong&gt;, picturesque coastal paths along the &lt;strong&gt;Orihuela Costa&lt;/strong&gt; with its coves and beaches, and unique routes through the historic &lt;strong&gt;Palmeral de Orihuela&lt;/strong&gt;, Europe's second-largest palm grove. The region's geography provides options from flat coastal walks to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Orihuela?
A: Yes, Orihuela has over 100 easy routes, making it very accessible for beginners and families. Many coastal paths, such as sections of the long coastal path from Playa Flamenca to Campoamor, offer relatively flat terrain and stunning views. The &lt;strong&gt;Palmeral de Orihuela&lt;/strong&gt; also provides gentle walks amidst its unique landscape.

Q: What are the most scenic natural features to explore on a hike in Orihuela?
A: Hikers can discover several scenic natural features. The &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Orihuela&lt;/strong&gt; offers dramatic limestone cliffs and panoramic views. The &lt;strong&gt;Orihuela Costa&lt;/strong&gt; boasts 16 kilometers of coastline with beautiful coves and beaches. Additionally, you can explore the unique dry riverbed landscape of &lt;strong&gt;Rio Seco Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt; or the distinctive pink hues of the &lt;strong&gt;Torrevieja Pink Salt Lake&lt;/strong&gt;, where you might even spot flamingos.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Orihuela?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e870515294/la-pedrera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pedrera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular trail offering views of the water and surrounding landscape. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1674667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de la Muela loop from Urbanización Montepinar&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a challenging ascent with rewarding vistas.

Q: What kind of landmarks or points of interest can I see along the trails?
A: Orihuela's trails offer access to various landmarks. A prominent destination is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/708814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de la Muela&lt;/a&gt;, a 465-meter-high viewpoint in the Sierra de Orihuela. You can also find historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5409706" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Conesa Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413822" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cox Castle&lt;/a&gt; nearby. The region's unique palm groves, the &lt;strong&gt;Palmeral de Orihuela&lt;/strong&gt;, are also a significant cultural and natural landmark.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Orihuela?
A: Orihuela benefits from a mild climate with over 320 sunny days a year, making it suitable for hiking year-round. However, the cooler months from autumn through spring (October to May) are generally more comfortable for longer hikes, avoiding the peak heat of summer. Even in winter, the weather is often pleasant for outdoor activities.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly hiking trails in Orihuela?
A: Many trails in Orihuela are suitable for dogs, especially those in natural parks and along the coast, provided they are kept on a leash and owners clean up after them. Always check local regulations for specific areas, but generally, the region's open spaces welcome well-behaved canine companions.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Orihuela?
A: There are nearly 200 hiking trails recorded in Orihuela, offering a wide variety of distances, difficulties, and landscapes. This includes over 100 easy routes, 77 moderate trails, and 20 more challenging options for experienced hikers.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Orihuela?
A: The hiking trails in Orihuela are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 600 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, from mountain views to coastal paths, and the variety of routes suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Orihuela?
A: While some trailheads, especially those closer to urban centers or coastal towns, might be accessible by local bus services, many of the more remote mountain or natural park trails may require private transport. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and routes in advance for specific starting points.

Q: Are there opportunities for advanced or challenging hikes in Orihuela?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Orihuela&lt;/strong&gt; offers more demanding routes with significant elevation gain. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10323752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de la Muela – Small Cave loop from Santuario Nuestra Senora del Pilar&lt;/a&gt; are rated as difficult, involving steep ascents to viewpoints like Cruz de la Muela, providing substantial climbs and rewarding panoramic views.
tips: The reservoir was built in the 1980s in order to regulate the supply of water for irrigation to the low-lying areas. Today it can be completely turned around and although it may be a bit long, the landscape is very picturesque. A part of the road goes through the Camino de Santiago Southeast Path of the South branch.

faqs.question: How difficult is the hike to La Pedrera Reservoir from Torremendo?
faqs.answer: The trail is officially rated as difficult, covering nearly 20 km with around 120 meters of elevation gain. While some sections require good fitness, other parts feature easily-accessible paths and are considered easy to medium terrain, suitable for various skill levels. Be prepared for a longer outing, estimated to take over 5 hours.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers varied terrain. You'll encounter a mix of designated paths (about 11.9 km), roads (6.61 km), and streets (1.05 km). Surfaces include approximately 7.15 km of unpaved sections, 6.61 km of asphalt, and some paved areas. Be ready for light winds and potentially changeable conditions.

faqs.question: What scenic views and natural features will I see?
faqs.answer: You can expect very picturesque scenery, especially around the La Pedrera Reservoir itself, known for its striking turquoise waters contrasting with the hilly countryside. The area is rich in flora, with olive, orange, and almond groves. You'll enjoy stunning mountain and lake views, particularly from well-equipped viewpoints that include benches for resting. It's also an excellent spot for birdwatching.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, a significant portion of this route, approximately 42%, coincides with the Camino del Sureste - Ramal Sur, which is part of the Camino de Santiago network. This offers a unique blend of local scenery and a historic pilgrimage path.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking in this region. Be mindful of changeable conditions and light winds, which can occur at any time.

faqs.question: Is the Ride to La Pedrera Reservoir from Torremendo trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the scenic views with your canine companion. However, always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, and be mindful of local regulations and other trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike this trail or access the La Pedrera Reservoir area. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail from Torremendo?
faqs.answer: Recent renovations have enhanced accessibility to the reservoir area, including a new car park. You can typically find parking near the reservoir itself, which serves as a convenient starting point for exploring the surrounding trails.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trail, like cafes or restaurants?
faqs.answer: The La Pedrera Reservoir area maintains a peaceful ambiance with limited amenities directly on the trail. For cafes, restaurants, or pubs, you would need to visit the nearby town of Torremendo.

faqs.question: What other activities are available at La Pedrera Reservoir?
faqs.answer: Beyond hiking, the La Pedrera Reservoir offers opportunities for other outdoor activities. Fishing is a popular pastime, and quad biking is also available in the area, providing different ways to experience the landscape.

faqs.question: Are there specific viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area around La Pedrera Reservoir features well-equipped viewpoints. These spots offer stunning mountain and lake views and often include benches, making them ideal for resting and enjoying the scenery, especially during sunset.
